description_long: This is a cylindrical fire hydrant with a domed top and a flanged base, finished in a weathered, off-white color with visible rust spots and stains. It features three prominent hose connection outlets, each capped and protruding from the main body at different angles. The hydrant's surface is rough and matte, with signs of age and exposure to the elements, including discoloration and minor surface pitting. The top of the hydrant has a central operating nut or cap, which is slightly raised and circular. There are no visible chains attached to the caps, and no manufacturer or model markings are discernible on the surface. The object is designed to be fixed in place, typically found on sidewalks or near curbs in urban environments.
description: A weathered, off-white cylindrical fire hydrant with three capped hose outlets, rust spots, and a domed top with a central nut.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main cylindrical body, domed top, and two hose outlets on either side, with rust and weathering visible.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the central front hose outlet and the side outlets, with the flanged base and surface stains more apparent.
description_view_2: Side view focusing on the two lateral hose outlets and the domed top, with less visibility of the front outlet.
description_view_3: Rear angled view showing the back and side outlets, with the cylindrical form and weathered surface details.
